sourceMovie.title: Twister
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Twister works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Jeremiah Johnson
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Twister clicked for you, Jeremiah Johnson (1972) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: San Andreas
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Twister clicked for you, San Andreas (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Mountain Between Us
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Twister clicked for you, The Mountain Between Us (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Twister?
faq.answer: Try Jeremiah Johnson, San Andreas, The Mountain Between Us—each shares DNA with Twister while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Twister?
faq.answer: Twister (1996) is best described as Action, Adventure, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Twister part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Twister Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
